theme_expansion.concept: A study of a dying garden where pressed, dried flowers are preserved behind layers of cracked, aged varnish and oxidized copper plating.
theme_expansion.narrative: The weapon serves as a relic from a forgotten conservatory. As the floral patterns transition from the stock to the barrel, they shift from vibrant, saturated pigments to desiccated, charcoal-toned skeletons, reflecting the passage of time and the fragility of nature against industrial steel.

elements.label: Focal Peony
elements.description: A large, semi-wilted peony flower with curling, translucent petal edges, rendered in fine-line ink illustration.

elements.label: Varnish Craquelure
elements.description: A macro texture of fine, spiderweb-like cracks in old lacquer, creating a sense of aged material depth.

elements.label: Barbed Tendrils
elements.description: Intertwining, thin, dry thorny vines that bridge the negative space between larger floral motifs.

elements.label: Petal Scatter
elements.description: Single, wind-blown, curling petals resting on the barrel area, showing signs of browning edges.

elements.label: Oxidized Frame
elements.description: Subtle, tarnished brass ornamental flourishes that outline the receiver edges.

elements.label: Skeletonized Foliage
elements.description: Brittle, translucent leaves showing prominent vein structures, layered behind flowers.

elements.label: Atmospheric Dust
elements.description: Micro-specks of light-colored debris scattered unevenly across the dark surfaces to imply a forgotten room.

elements.label: Stained Ink Wash
elements.description: Soft, monochromatic ink bleeding patterns that transition between the botanical elements.

world_elements: A large, semi-wilted peony flower with curling, translucent petal edges, rendered in fine-line ink illustration.
A macro texture of fine, spiderweb-like cracks in old lacquer, creating a sense of aged material depth.
Intertwining, thin, dry thorny vines that bridge the negative space between larger floral motifs.
Single, wind-blown, curling petals resting on the barrel area, showing signs of browning edges.
Subtle, tarnished brass ornamental flourishes that outline the receiver edges.
Brittle, translucent leaves showing prominent vein structures, layered behind flowers.
Micro-specks of light-colored debris scattered unevenly across the dark surfaces to imply a forgotten room.
Soft, monochromatic ink bleeding patterns that transition between the botanical elements.
small symbolic objects related to the main theme
environmental fragments that imply a complete world
fine material grain and ornamental micro-detail
evaluation_criteria: Floral elements must feel organic and hand-illustrated rather than procedurally generated.
The contrast between the 'fresh' botanical colors and the 'aged' varnish must be legible at a distance.
The density of the pattern must successfully decline from stock to barrel to avoid visual noise at the muzzle.
theme_expansion.reference_policy: Strictly adheres to historical botanical illustration styles and classical ink wash techniques; no references to living digital artists.

directions.title: Archival Sepia Study
directions.concept: Focus on the monochromatic, dried-out state of a historical herbarium.
materials: glossy varnish overlay with heavy crackle
directions.hero_strategy: Dense multi-scale theme artwork whose large crops remain visually complete.
directions.secondary_strategy: Distribute supporting motifs and material detail across the full canvas.
directions.connector_strategy: Use continuous line, colour, and texture rhythm rather than component stickers.
directions.background_strategy: Resolve the background as an active material field with no large dead zones.
directions.route_a_logic: Brief: flower. Style: A somber, archival-grade botanical study where vibrant life meets inevitable decomposition, preserved under layers of translucent, fractured lacquer.. Direction: Focus on the monochromatic, dried-out state of a historical herbarium.. Visual language: fine-line pen work, cross-hatched shadows, sepia-toned ink washes, spiderweb craquelure, desiccated vein structures, tarnished brass etchings. Motifs: skeleton leaves, ink smudges, dust motes. Palette: #2D3A2C, #8C7B75, #C4A484, #4A2E35, #D9D0C1. Material cues: matte parchment texture, glossy varnish overlay with heavy crackle, patinated brass metallic highlights, absorbent paper grain. Route A objective: Establish a seamless botanical tapestry that flows longitudinally across the weapon geometry.. tiling density distribution scaled to 0.75; no visible seam-lines; uniform vine interconnection. Emphasis: Densely packed leaf skeletons. Required: maximum of three color overlaps, no hero flowers crossing component boundaries. Avoid: bright saturated neons, vectorized sharp edges, digital gradient blurs, photorealistic petal textures, large repeating geometric shapes.
directions.route_b_logic: Brief: flower. Style: A somber, archival-grade botanical study where vibrant life meets inevitable decomposition, preserved under layers of translucent, fractured lacquer.. Direction: Focus on the monochromatic, dried-out state of a historical herbarium.. Visual language: fine-line pen work, cross-hatched shadows, sepia-toned ink washes, spiderweb craquelure, desiccated vein structures, tarnished brass etchings. Motifs: skeleton leaves, ink smudges, dust motes. Palette: #2D3A2C, #8C7B75, #C4A484, #4A2E35, #D9D0C1. Material cues: matte parchment texture, glossy varnish overlay with heavy crackle, patinated brass metallic highlights, absorbent paper grain. Route B objective: generate one complete, dense, crop-robust master artwork, fit it across continuous left/right/top weapon space, then automatically cut and repack it through the OBJ-derived UV atlas. Distribute useful visual information at multiple scales across the full image; avoid large empty regions. Subject-part placement on named weapon components is not required or evaluated. Avoid: bright saturated neons, vectorized sharp edges, digital gradient blurs, photorealistic petal textures, large repeating geometric shapes.
directions.recommendation_reason: Distinct Archival Sepia Study interpretation with explicit Route A and crop-robust master-artwork Route B logic.

artwork_candidates.title: Narrative Panorama
artwork_candidates.variation: environment-led panorama with several separated story clusters, small figures or creatures, symbolic objects, layered foreground/midground/background, and rhythmic atmospheric connections
artwork_candidates.prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept. Asset type: game weapon-skin source for later UV-aware composition. Generate one original dense landscape master artwork, approximately 16:9, for the Withered Petal Covenant weapon-skin theme. Theme content: A large, semi-wilted peony flower with curling, translucent petal edges, rendered in fine-line ink illustration.; A macro texture of fine, spiderweb-like cracks in old lacquer, creating a sense of aged material depth.; Intertwining, thin, dry thorny vines that bridge the negative space between larger floral motifs.; Single, wind-blown, curling petals resting on the barrel area, showing signs of browning edges.; Subtle, tarnished brass ornamental flourishes that outline the receiver edges.; Brittle, translucent leaves showing prominent vein structures, layered behind flowers.; Micro-specks of light-colored debris scattered unevenly across the dark surfaces to imply a forgotten room.; Soft, monochromatic ink bleeding patterns that transition between the botanical elements.. Narrative: The weapon serves as a relic from a forgotten conservatory. As the floral patterns transition from the stock to the barrel, they shift from vibrant, saturated pigments to desiccated, charcoal-toned skeletons, reflecting the passage of time and the fragility of nature against industrial steel.. Theme enrichment notes: The design utilizes a heavy 'aged lacquer' overlay that creates a high-gloss finish over the matte floral ink work.; Floral density should be highest on the receiver and decrease as the pattern moves toward the muzzle.; Avoid solid geometric repeats; favor an organic, asymmetrical arrangement that mimics an overgrown garden wall.. Style: A somber, archival-grade botanical study where vibrant life meets inevitable decomposition, preserved under layers of translucent, fractured lacquer.. Visual language: fine-line pen work, cross-hatched shadows, sepia-toned ink washes, spiderweb craquelure, desiccated vein structures, tarnished brass etchings. Palette: #2D3A2C, #8C7B75, #C4A484, #4A2E35, #D9D0C1. Fill the complete canvas with layered, high-quality, high-density visual information at several scales. Every large crop must remain attractive and richly detailed. Build a complete thematic world with many medium and small clusters: primary forms, related symbolic objects, environment, architecture or nature, atmospheric connectors, linework, and material texture. No single subject may dominate more than roughly 35% of the canvas width or height, and no subject may stretch across the full image width. Weave these elements through every region rather than arranging isolated stickers on empty space. Do not split it into component assets and do not reserve large plain or near-black areas. This image will be fitted to continuous weapon space and automatically cut into the OBJ-derived UV atlas. Do not draw a weapon, weapon mockup, UV layout, text, logo, watermark, frame, presentation board, or product render. Locked candidate art direction: Decay Gradient (candidate_04_dir_04_decay_gradient). Concept: Visual metaphor for time: vibrant flora on stock, decaying to dust at muzzle.. Motifs: dying peony, fading ink, scattered petals. Expanded thematic world: Focal Peony, Varnish Craquelure, Barbed Tendrils, Petal Scatter, Oxidized Frame, Skeletonized Foliage, Atmospheric Dust, Stained Ink Wash, A large, semi-wilted peony flower with curling, translucent petal edges, rendered in fine-line ink illustration., A macro texture of fine, spiderweb-like cracks in old lacquer, creating a sense of aged material depth., Intertwining, thin, dry thorny vines that bridge the negative space between larger floral motifs., Single, wind-blown, curling petals resting on the barrel area, showing signs of browning edges., Subtle, tarnished brass ornamental flourishes that outline the receiver edges., Brittle, translucent leaves showing prominent vein structures, layered behind flowers., Micro-specks of light-colored debris scattered unevenly across the dark surfaces to imply a forgotten room., Soft, monochromatic ink bleeding patterns that transition between the botanical elements., small symbolic objects related to the main theme, environmental fragments that imply a complete world, atmospheric connectors and motion rhythms, fine material grain and ornamental micro-detail, peony bloom, thorny vine, skeleton leaf, curling petal, dust mote cloud, oxidized scrollwork, fine-line pen work, cross-hatched shadows, sepia-toned ink washes, spiderweb craquelure, desiccated vein structures, tarnished brass etchings, matte parchment texture, glossy varnish overlay with heavy crackle, patinated brass metallic highlights, absorbent paper grain. Route-B emphasis: Brief: flower. Style: A somber, archival-grade botanical study where vibrant life meets inevitable decomposition, preserved under layers of translucent, fractured lacquer.. Direction: Visual metaphor for time: vibrant flora on stock, decaying to dust at muzzle.. Visual language: fine-line pen work, cross-hatched shadows, sepia-toned ink washes, spiderweb craquelure, desiccated vein structures, tarnished brass etchings. Motifs: dying peony, fading ink, scattered petals. Palette: #2D3A2C, #8C7B75, #C4A484, #4A2E35, #D9D0C1. Material cues: matte parchment texture, glossy varnish overlay with heavy crackle, patinated brass metallic highlights, absorbent paper grain. Route B objective: generate one complete, dense, crop-robust master artwork, fit it across continuous left/right/top weapon space, then automatically cut and repack it through the OBJ-derived UV atlas. Distribute useful visual information at multiple scales across the full image; avoid large empty regions. Subject-part placement on named weapon components is not required or evaluated. Avoid: bright saturated neons, vectorized sharp edges, digital gradient blurs, photorealistic petal textures, large repeating geometric shapes.. Do not drift to another art direction. CRITICAL SOURCE-ASSET MODE: all weapon names, component names, and placement descriptions above are placement metadata only for later composition. No weapon or weapon part may appear. Do not depict, imply, crop, silhouette, emboss, or texture any firearm, weapon, receiver, trigger, grip, magazine, stock, barrel, muzzle, mechanical weapon part, weapon mockup, product mockup, UV layout, rectangular material sample, framed tile, or presentation board. COMPOSITION-GROUP CONTRACT: preserve the requested spatial structure and narrative purpose exactly; spanning sources must read as one continuous elongated subject, grouped sources as coordinated reusable units, and independent sources as one component-specific mark. MASTER-ARTWORK CONTRACT: create one dense, opaque, edge-to-edge landscape thematic artwork with strong local detail at multiple scales. Large crops from any part of the image must remain visually rich. Use many medium and small subject clusters rather than one oversized subject: no single element may occupy more than roughly 35% of the canvas width or height, and no subject may span the complete image width. Weave primary motifs, supporting symbols, environmental fragments, atmospheric connectors, linework, and material texture through every region. Do not isolate motifs as separate stickers and do not leave large plain or near-black negative-space pockets. Do not draw a weapon, weapon part, mockup, UV layout, text, logo, watermark, border, framed panel, presentation board, or product render. Subject-part placement is not evaluated. Output only the landscape 2D source artwork, approximately 16:9, with no text, logo, watermark, border, annotation, or explanation.
CANDIDATE VARIATION 1: environment-led panorama with several separated story clusters, small figures or creatures, symbolic objects, layered foreground/midground/background, and rhythmic atmospheric connections. Create a materially different composition from the other candidates while preserving the locked direction and palette. Include at least ten visually distinct clusters distributed across the landscape canvas. Keep the main theme readable through repeated medium/small appearances and related elements, not one giant central subject.

artwork_candidates.prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept. Asset type: game weapon-skin source for later UV-aware composition. Generate one original dense landscape master artwork, approximately 16:9, for the Withered Petal Covenant weapon-skin theme. Theme content: A large, semi-wilted peony flower with curling, translucent petal edges, rendered in fine-line ink illustration.; A macro texture of fine, spiderweb-like cracks in old lacquer, creating a sense of aged material depth.; Intertwining, thin, dry thorny vines that bridge the negative space between larger floral motifs.; Single, wind-blown, curling petals resting on the barrel area, showing signs of browning edges.; Subtle, tarnished brass ornamental flourishes that outline the receiver edges.; Brittle, translucent leaves showing prominent vein structures, layered behind flowers.; Micro-specks of light-colored debris scattered unevenly across the dark surfaces to imply a forgotten room.; Soft, monochromatic ink bleeding patterns that transition between the botanical elements.. Narrative: The weapon serves as a relic from a forgotten conservatory. As the floral patterns transition from the stock to the barrel, they shift from vibrant, saturated pigments to desiccated, charcoal-toned skeletons, reflecting the passage of time and the fragility of nature against industrial steel..
